title: Canonical Feature Slice Naming — NXGN Actions
purpose: Define the canonical naming vocabulary for API, BFF-proxy, and frontend feature slices so implementation, plans, and lex entities use the same verbs and nouns across the platform.

rules.rule_text: Use the canonical backend slice verbs `Get`, `GetGridList`, `GetLookup`/`GetLookupList`, `Save`, `Delete`, `Assign`, and `Unassign` unless the authoritative domain contract clearly dictates a different verb phrase.
Keep lex entities, plans, beads, file paths, namespaces, classes, and tests aligned to the same canonical slice names.
When proxying or mirroring Identity, reuse Identity's authoritative noun/verb vocabulary for the server-side slice names and contract-facing routes.
Frontend service methods may use ergonomic names like `getGridList()` and `getLookup()` while still mapping to canonical backend slice names.
If naming drift is discovered while a module is still shallow, rename immediately rather than preserving the drift for compatibility.
Do not invent local synonyms such as `ListRoles` when the canonical slice is `GetRolesGridList`.
Do not treat a legacy module's drift as precedent for new module implementation.

sections.title: Scope
content: This pattern applies to all feature slices in NxGN Actions:
- frontend feature services, page components, and route files
- lex plans, beads, feature areas, endpoint entities, and design notes
It exists because legacy names from one module must not silently become the convention for the next module.

sections.title: Canonical Vocabulary
content: - `Get` for a single entity or projection
- `GetLookup` or `GetLookupList` for lookup/dropdown reads, following the authoritative downstream/domain contract
- `Assign` / `Unassign` for membership-style mutations when the domain uses those verbs
The server-side slice/class names and lex entities should use the canonical backend verbs even when the frontend service methods are shorter.

sections.title: Authoritative Source Rule
content: When a feature mirrors or proxies an authoritative downstream domain such as NxGN Identity, Actions must reuse that domain's canonical noun/verb vocabulary for the server-side slice names and lex references.
- Identity-style `GetRolesGridListEndpoint` is authoritative for the roles grid slice; Actions should not invent `ListRolesEndpoint`
- Identity-style `AssignUserToRole` / `UnassignUsersFromRole` are authoritative for role membership mutations
- Identity-style `/permissions/lookup` is authoritative for permission catalogue routes; local docs must not drift to `/permissions`
A legacy local name in an older module is technical debt, not precedent.

sections.title: Good and Bad Examples
content: Plan/bead says GetRolesGridList, implementation says ListRoles
This creates traceability drift and makes reviews unreliable.
